Off Calendar Sep-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Ansel Halliburton of the law offices of Kronenberger Rosenfeld (415-955-1155 x 122) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFFS ROSEBANK ROAD MEDICAL SERVICES LTD., GEETA GANESH'S Motion For Order Authorizing Disclosure Of Subscriber Information By Cable Company is GRANTED. On its own motion and per Le Francois v. Goel (2010) 35 Cal. 4th 1094, the court has decided to consider the motion of plaintiffs as an invitation to address the issue of the discoverability of the identity of the user and account holder for the IP address that was used to send the Disputed Review without regard to whether the current motion complies with the requirements of CCP 1008. On its own motion, the court has determined that, based on the papers submitted on plaintiffs' current motion, plaintiffs have shown that the identity of the user and account holder for the IP address that was used to send the Disputed Review satisfies the standard of discovery relevance because it seeks the identity of a person who sent an anonymous post unfavorable to plaintiffs to the same website as the anonymous posts unfavorable to plaintiffs that are the subject of this case. The identity of the user and account holder for the IP address that was used to send the Disputed Review will likely yield probative information on the issue of whether or not defendant Ramji Govindarajan is or is not the person who sent the posts that are the subject of this case. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

APPEARANCES - ATTY. JEFFREY M. ROSENFELD OF THE KROENBERGER/ROSENFELD LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-955-1155 EXT. 112 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOSEPH C. EDMONDSON, TEL. NO. 1-503-701-9719 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Ramji Govindarajan's motion to compel discovery is granted in part. This order tracks the categories listed in the judge pro tem's report. As to interrogatory 4 and document request 13 to plaintiff Rosebank Road Medical Service Ltd, Rosebank must provide the names and contact information for the people it contends stopped patronizing the medical facility as a result of the alleged defamatory postings and who viewed the alleged defamatory postings, but Mr. Govindarajan may only use that information for this case and may not provide that information to anyone other those persons who he or his counsel has retained to work on this case. As to interrogatory 4 to plaintiff Geeta Ganesh and RFAs 4-6 and corresponding form interrogatory 17.1, no further responses are required. The information sought implicates Ms. Ganesh's privacy and is not directly relevant to the claims alleged in this case. As to interrogatories 10-11, no further responses are required. The information sought does not meet the standard of discovery relevance. As to document requests 3 and 10, Rosebank must provide all responsive information, but Mr. Govindarajan may only use that information for this case and may not provide that information to anyone other those persons who he or his counsel has retained to work on this case. As to form interrogatory 2.5, no further response is required. All further responses must be verified and must be provided no later than June 9, 2017. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =302/hk

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jan-27-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Jeffrey M. Rosenfeld of the law offices of Kronenberger|rosenfeld (415.955.1155 ext. 112) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Frear Stephen Schmid of the law offices of Frear Stephen Schmid (415.788.5957) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANT RAMJI GOVINDARAJAN'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is DENIED without prejudice. Mr. Govindarajan has not established that at the time plaintiffs filed their original complaint they knew both Mr. Govindarajan's identity and facts linking him to the reviews that form the basis of their complaint. (Fuller v. Tucker (2000) 84 Cal.App.4th 1163, 1170; Streicher v. Tommy's Electric Co. (1985) 164 Cal.App.3d 876, 883.) The Indian criminal police report, which only indicates Dr. Ganesh's suspicion of Mr. Govindarajan's involvement, communications from Dr. Ganesh's husband, and the discovery responses linking Mr. Govindarjan to the reviews, did not occur until after the original complaint was filed. Plaintiffs subsequent amendment of their complaint and substitution of Mr. Govindarajan for John Doe 1 consequently complied with CCP 474. Court grants 20 days leave to file responsive pleading. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

Appearances: Jeffrey Rosenfeld of Kronenberger Rosenfeld LLP telephone number 415-955-1155 for Plaintiffs Rosebank Road Medical Services Ltd. and Geeta Ganesh.
Joseph Edmonson of Law Offices of J. Curtis Edmonson telephone number 415-955-1155 appeared by telephone for Defendant John Doe 1.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JOHN DOE 1'S Motion To Quash Summons And Complaint is denied. Assuming without deciding that a forum nonconveniens motion may be made prior to service of a summon, defendant has failed to show that he is amenable to jurisdiction in New Zealand and thus he has not satisfied his threshold burden of showing that New Zealand is a suitable alternative forum. Moreover, defendant has not presented any facts showing that California is inconvenient to any of the parties or the witnesses and the evidence submitted by the plaintiffs shows otherwise. Order signed in open Court.
